values.value: Sensitive Receptors and Section 106 Initial Scoping - {ProjectName}

Sensitive receptor and cultural resource scoping triggered by EPA NEPAssist Places layer detection ({LayerLabel}). Document all schools, hospitals, places of worship, and other sensitive receptors within 0.5 miles of the project APE to evaluate localized noise, air quality, and traffic impacts. For any National Register of Historic Places (NRHP) points detected, initiate NHPA Section 106 consultation: prepare Area of Potential Effect (APE) definition memorandum, identify and notify interested parties (SHPO, THPO, tribes), and complete the Section 106 four-step consultation process per 36 CFR Part 800. Note: detailed archaeological site locations must be obtained directly from SHPO — public endpoints do not carry site-specific sensitive data.
